caveats: a: The BLS table covers NAICS 335100, including commercial and other lighting products, rather than residential fixtures alone.
a: Occupation shares are not task shares.
a: Domestic manufacturers range from design-led assemblers to vertically integrated metalworking and finishing plants.
rho: No direct task-level AI adoption evidence was available for residential fixture manufacturers.
rho: Design-led firms may realize more substitution than plants with a high share of direct labor.
e: Capture depends on brand strength, channel mix, inventory discipline, outsourced versus in-house production, and whether saved time translates to fewer costs or simply more product variants.
s5: LED adoption evidence covers residential and commercial markets and predates the run date.
s5: Substitution differs between portable decorative lamps, builder-grade fixtures, and integrated smart luminaires.
q: No direct customer-retention data were available.
q: Quality is likely higher for branded specification relationships and lower for private-label and promotional retail programs.
d5: Monthly housing data are volatile and include multifamily units.
d5: Installed lighting units are not annual fixture sales.
d5: Domestic manufacturer revenue can diverge from end-market demand because of import share and channel inventory changes.
o: Asset-light import-and-assemble models may consolidate more roles than vertically integrated plants.
o: Small firms may already have highly combined management functions.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Product revenue from homebuilders, electrical and lighting distributors, retailers, e-commerce channels, designers, remodelers, and homeowners. Repeat demand comes from new construction, renovation, style refreshes, replacement, and product-line introductions; branded and specified products can earn better margins than private-label or commodity fixtures.
lens.excluded_activities: Commercial, industrial, institutional, roadway, and traffic lighting; LED semiconductor and bulb manufacturing; glass and plastic shade manufacturing; ceiling fans and bath fans with integrated lighting; current-carrying wiring devices; importers, distributors, retailers, installers, and design firms without principal domestic fixture-manufacturing activity; and captive divisions that are not independently marketable businesses.
lens.included_activities: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of fixed and portable residential electric lighting fixtures and metal, paper, or textile lamp shades, including indoor and outdoor ceiling, wall, pendant, recessed, track, table, floor, low-voltage, solar, decorative, and residential grow-light fixtures.

a.bridge: Residential fixture manufacturers use the same core design, engineering, sourcing, production, sales, and administrative functions represented in the 335100 occupation mix. Residential products may carry somewhat more styling and retail-channel work than the broader group, so the proxy is applied with a wide range rather than as direct six-digit evidence.
caveats: The BLS table covers NAICS 335100, including commercial and other lighting products, rather than residential fixtures alone.
Occupation shares are not task shares.
Domestic manufacturers range from design-led assemblers to vertically integrated metalworking and finishing plants.
a.change_evidence: Multimodal design, visualization, document, forecasting, and customer-service systems broaden assistance across product development and commercial workflows, but fixtures still require prototyping and production.
a.rationale: BLS data for broader electric lighting equipment manufacturing show production occupations at 42.42% of employment, while management, business and financial, computer, engineering, design, sales, and office-support groups together form a substantial knowledge-work layer. Residential fixture makers expose product design, CAD, rendering, sourcing, pricing, demand planning, marketing, specification, customer support, and administration to AI, while fabrication, assembly, finishing, inspection, packaging, and materials handling remain physical.

caveats: Monthly housing data are volatile and include multifamily units.
Installed lighting units are not annual fixture sales.
Domestic manufacturer revenue can diverge from end-market demand because of import share and channel inventory changes.
d5.change_evidence: Census reported June 2026 housing starts at a 1.427 million annual rate, but single-family starts were 895,000 and essentially flat from May. DOE estimated 6.5 billion residential lighting units in 2020, supporting a large replacement base while documenting rapid LED penetration.
d5.rationale: A large installed base, renovation and replacement, household formation, and ongoing housing construction support demand, while integrated LEDs and connected controls create mix opportunities. High interest rates, housing cyclicality, slow single-family activity, import competition, and longer LED lifetimes restrain unit growth.

caveats: Capture depends on brand strength, channel mix, inventory discipline, outsourced versus in-house production, and whether saved time translates to fewer costs or simply more product variants.
e.change_evidence: Digital product-development and commerce workflows make deployment practical, but competitive price points and easy comparison push some gains to customers.
e.rationale: Independent manufacturers can retain productivity through faster collection development, more design variants, lower rendering and documentation cost, better sourcing and inventory decisions, and reduced sales-support and administrative burden. Retail and builder bargaining power, private-label competition, software costs, and the need for sampling and compliance work prevent full capture.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) at ancestor 3351: labor compensation (L02) $3,249.0M / sectoral output (T30) $10,230.1M, year 2024

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $422,130 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 3351); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: Asset-light import-and-assemble models may consolidate more roles than vertically integrated plants.
Small firms may already have highly combined management functions.
o.change_evidence: The broad lighting occupation mix combines a sizeable office and professional layer with 42% production employment, preserving a clear operating core even as digital workflows improve.
o.rationale: AI can consolidate some product-development, marketing, planning, support, and administrative work, but manufacturers still need accountable leadership across design approval, product safety, sourcing, factory operations, quality, channel management, and working capital. Organizational displacement is therefore limited.

caveats: No direct customer-retention data were available.
Quality is likely higher for branded specification relationships and lower for private-label and promotional retail programs.
q.change_evidence: Connected and integrated LED products can deepen ecosystem and compatibility ties, while digital marketplaces and AI-assisted design lower discovery and product-development barriers for rivals.
q.rationale: Brand, design language, retailer and distributor placement, builder specifications, catalog breadth, reliable availability, safety compliance, and matching product families can create repeat purchasing and shelf or specification persistence. Switching remains easy in many decorative and builder-grade categories, with intense price and style competition and abundant imports.

caveats: No direct task-level AI adoption evidence was available for residential fixture manufacturers.
Design-led firms may realize more substitution than plants with a high share of direct labor.
rho.change_evidence: Improving image-to-design and engineering assistants raise substitution potential in early product-development and merchandising tasks, while electrical safety, manufacturability, and physical aesthetics preserve human review.
rho.rationale: AI can materially augment concept generation, rendering, CAD support, bill-of-material and supplier analysis, product-copy creation, forecasting, quoting, order support, and administrative work. It is less able to replace physical design validation, safety and performance testing, tooling, fabrication, finishing, assembly, and final quality control.

caveats: LED adoption evidence covers residential and commercial markets and predates the run date.
Substitution differs between portable decorative lamps, builder-grade fixtures, and integrated smart luminaires.
s5.change_evidence: DOE found LEDs at roughly 48% of the residential and commercial installed base in 2020, reflecting a rapid technology transition that raises fixture life and shifts value toward integrated electronics and controls.
s5.rationale: Residential spaces continue to require physical luminaires, so pure digital substitution is limited. Risk comes from integrated smart-home products, modular LED systems, overseas direct-to-consumer brands, and longer-lived LED fixtures reducing replacement frequency. Connected controls and efficient integrated luminaires can also expand value per fixture rather than eliminate the category.

principal_driver: A large residential installed base combined with renovation, housing formation, and premiumization toward integrated and connected fixtures.
principal_weakness: Cyclical housing exposure and low switching costs in a highly import- and style-competitive product market.

sources.supports: Broader lighting-industry occupation mix used as the task-exposure proxy.
